operating_state: Net sales increased 11% to $13.37 billion in Q2 2022 driven by price realization and higher shipment volumes across all segments. Trade receivables increased $2.05 billion during six months primarily due to seasonal patterns and elevated demand, with broad geographic strength across Latin America and Western Europe.
cost_structure: Cost of sales ratio deteriorated to 74.1% in Q2 from 72.1% prior year as material cost inflation outpaced pricing actions. Research and development expenses increased 20% in Q2 reflecting continued technology investment, while selling and administrative expenses rose 11% driven by higher provision for credit losses and inflationary pressures. Inventories increased $2.99 billion year-over-year due to elevated demand and supply chain disruptions.
material_events: Completed acquisition of majority ownership in Kreisel Electric and three former Deere-Hitachi excavator factories, generating $326 million non-cash gain on previously held equity investment. UAW collective bargaining agreement included ratification bonus and 10% wage increase, while company made $1 billion voluntary contribution to U.S. OPEB plan. Share repurchases totaled $1.23 billion for six months with quarterly dividend increased 8% to $1.13 per share.
macro_exposures: Russia-Ukraine conflict resulted in $120 million pretax expense in Q2 including asset impairments and credit loss reserves. Supply chain disruptions continue impacting production levels through industry capacity constraints, material availability, and global logistics delays. Currency translation adjustments resulted in $515 million negative impact on comprehensive income due to strengthening U.S. dollar.
forward_outlook: Industry sales of large agricultural machinery expected up significantly in U.S./Canada and South America, with moderate growth in Europe and decline in Asia, while construction equipment sales in U.S./Canada expected up moderately. Capital expenditures for equipment operations estimated at approximately $1.175 billion for 2022. Financial services net income expected slightly lower than fiscal 2021 due to higher provision for credit losses.
uncertainty_profile: Credit loss allowances increased to $190 million primarily due to Russia-Ukraine impacts and higher portfolio balances. Pension obligations remeasured due to UAW agreement, decreasing funded status by $495 million and increasing future pension expense annually. Financial services warehouse facility requires renewal by November 2022, while failure to meet sustainability targets on 2029 notes will result in interest rate increase from April 2026.
